design_philosophy.core_loop: Select plays, execute team basketball strategy, manage player stamina and positioning, score more points than opponent across four quarters
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time decision-making between offensive play execution and defensive positioning while managing fatigue across roster
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Controlling an NBA team with authentic roster management, experiencing strategic basketball simulation with tactical depth
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: Isometric perspective designed for strategic overview rather than arcade spectacle; emphasis on coaching/management decisions (players can take roles as players, coaches, or GMs) over reflexive action; roster limitations (Genesis version restricted trades to starting lineup only, no Create-a-Player) enforce constraints-based strategy rather than customization

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Multi-role gameplay (player/coach/GM modes) allows players to engage at different strategic abstraction levels; multiplayer noted as variable difficulty depending on opponent skill

progression.upgrade_system: Roster management through limited trades; Genesis version restricted to starting lineup trades only
replayability_hooks: Different rosters across platform versions (1993-94 on SNES vs 1994-95 on Genesis)

world_building_techniques: Authentic 1994 NBA rosters and player statistics
Cover features 1994 NBA Finals action shot establishing temporal setting
Roster-specific team identities and player attributes

faction_indicators: NBA team affiliation through roster selection
Role selection (player vs coach vs GM) indicates engagement philosophy
environmental_storytelling: Court perspective and UI convey professional basketball simulation authenticity
Roster composition reflects real 1994-95 NBA landscape
